revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and its is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg||Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Musa Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/okayama-history/13musa-ohtsuka-kofun.htm Okayama City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260850.pdfOkayama Prefecture home page]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Musa Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/okayama-history/13musa-ohtsuka-kofun.htm Okayama City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260850.pdfOkayama Prefecture home page]

The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Musa Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/okayama-history/13musa-ohtsuka-kofun.htm Okayama City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260850.pdfOkayama Prefecture home page]

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Musa Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/okayama-history/13musa-ohtsuka-kofun.htm Okayama City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260850.pdfOkayama Prefecture home page]

The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[:en:San'yōdō|San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Musa Otsuka Kofun]][[en:Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11570258]] Wikidata name: Musa Otsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.725225|N|133.9768|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[:en:San'yōdō|San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[:en:San'yōdō|San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Musa Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/okayama-history/13musa-ohtsuka-kofun.htm Okayama City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260850.pdfOkayama Prefecture home page]

==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the [[:en:San'yōdō|San'yōdō]] highway crosses the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]]. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of [[:en:Granite|Granite]] and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the {{ill|San'yōdō|en|San'yōdō|WD=Q1376722|lt=San'yōdō}} highway crosses the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|WD=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River}}. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =牟佐大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Musa Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Musa Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.725225|N|133.9768|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|30.81|N|133|58|36.48|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the {{ill|San'yōdō|en|San'yōdō|WD=Q1376722|lt=San'yōdō}} highway crosses the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|WD=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River}}. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>

It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the {{ill|San'yōdō|en|San'yōdō|WD=Q1376722|lt=San'yōdō|1=San'yōdō}} highway crosses the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|WD=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River|1=Asahi River}}. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Musa Ōtsuka Kofun'''|牟佐大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Musa neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|qid=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2261|title=牟佐大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Musa Ōtsuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 40 meters and a height of 10 meters, and a huge tunnel-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a total length of 18 meters with the opening orientated to the east. It is situated at a strategic location where the ancient route of the {{ill|San'yōdō|en|San'yōdō|qid=Q1376722|lt=San'yōdō|1=San'yōdō}} highway crosses the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|qid=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River|1=Asahi River}}. The burial chamber is made of megalithic blocks of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} and contains a hollowed-out house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} measuring 2.88 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.5 meters high. The sarcophagus is made of shell limestone quarried at Mount Namigata in [[:en:Ibara, Okayama|Ibara, Okayama]]. The sarcophagus has a hole, from which its {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were robbed in antiquity. It is estimated that the tumulus was built at the end of the 6th century from the shape of the sarcophagus, and it is speculated that this was a mausoleum of the Kibi no Kamimichi clan, a powerful ruling family in ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D computer rendition of the burial chamber and passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Opening File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Musa Otsuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
